items.summary: In backend/main.mo, declare the owner principal as a stable let constant (not a mutable stable var) initialized with Principal.fromText("q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe"). This value must never be overwritten by any upgrade, migration, or role-management call. Remove any stable var or TrieMap entry that could shadow or replace the owner principal.

In backend/main.mo, rewrite the hasPermission helper so that #owner checks are a direct equality comparison between the caller and the stable owner constant (REQ-48) and never fall through to the roles TrieMap. The roles TrieMap is only consulted for #authorized and #admin role checks. Ensure no code path can grant #owner via the TrieMap.

Audit and fix every owner-guarded backend endpoint (setStripeKey, getStripeKey, addAuthorizedPrincipal, removeAuthorizedPrincipal, listAuthorizedPrincipals, createLaborRate, updateLaborRate, deleteLaborRate, createPackage, updatePackage, deletePackage, and any other #owner-only function) to use the hardened hasPermission check from REQ-49. Ensure none of these functions accept the roles TrieMap as a fallback for the owner check.

Ensure all standard CRUD endpoints (createClient, updateClient, deleteClient, createJob, updateJob, deleteJob, updateJobStatus, createPart, updatePart, deletePart, usePartOnJob, addJobPhoto, removeJobPhoto, addLaborLineItem, removeLaborLineItem, updateJobSchedule, updateJobPayment, createPaymentIntent, storeUserSignature) accept calls from any principal that passes hasPermission(caller, #authorized) — which includes both the owner principal and any TrieMap-registered #authorized principal. This supports multiple technician/admin users with full CRUD access.

In the frontend Settings page (SettingsPage.tsx), update the owner-check logic that gates the Settings screen. The frontend isOwner check must compare the authenticated Internet Identity principal against the hardcoded string 'q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe'. Do not rely solely on a backend role query that could return a stale or missing result. If the backend getMyRole (or equivalent) query is used, also verify locally. The Settings screen must render fully for the owner principal.

Add a migration guard in backend/main.mo (or migration.mo if needed) to preserve the stable owner principal across all future upgrades. On upgrade, if any logic would reset or overwrite the ownerPrincipal value, abort or skip that step. Document the stable owner principal in a comment so future builds do not accidentally replace it.
